stories.headline: US-Iran signing slips past Sunday deadline; oil holds a two-month low as a G7 deal is eyed
stories.summary: The US-Iran memorandum that President Trump said was 'scheduled to get signed' Sunday June 14 to reopen the Strait of Hormuz did not materialize; Iran's foreign ministry said it 'will not be tomorrow' but could come 'in the coming days,' possibly at the G7 summit in France (June 15-17). A senior US official kept the odds near 80%. With markets closed, crude held Friday's roughly two-month-low settles of WTI $84.88 and Brent $87.33.

perspectives.keyFraming: A missed Sunday signing keeps a residual war premium in oil; the action now shifts to the G7 in France.
perspectives.summary: NPR and Bloomberg reported the deal Trump touted for Sunday did not get signed, with Tehran pushing the timeline into 'the coming days' and officials pointing to the G7 summit as a venue. Crude held Friday's two-month-low settles (WTI $84.88, Brent $87.33) over the weekend, and analysts stressed a physical flow recovery would lag any signature by weeks to months.

sources.title: Trump says deal to end Iran war will be signed Sunday, as Iran disagrees on timing
US, Iran Edge Toward Interim Deal Signing Close to G7 Next Week

perspectives.keyFraming: Maximum pressure is closing in on a settlement: a reopened Hormuz would unwind the oil-driven inflation shock.
perspectives.summary: Right-leaning coverage framed the prospective accord as vindication of Trump's pressure campaign, with the strait set to reopen, sanctions and frozen-funds relief as the trade, and falling pump prices easing the worst inflation in three years just as the Fed meets.

sources.title: US-Iran deal will likely be signed by the weekend in Geneva – Bloomberg

perspectives.keyFraming: Repeated victory laps risk a market head-fake; Tehran holds the leverage and keeps adding conditions.
perspectives.summary: Left-leaning analysis warned Iran's denials and reservations about US 'instability' mean a signing is far from assured, and that a stalled deal could snap oil back higher just as energy-driven inflation bites households and complicates the Fed's path.

sources.title: Qatari Negotiators Travel To Tehran In Bid To Finalize US-Iran Deal

stories.headline: Wall Street ends Friday near records on Iran-peace hopes and SpaceX debut; week ahead pivots to Warsh's first FOMC
stories.summary: The S&P 500 rose 0.5% to 7,431.46, the Nasdaq added 0.31% to 25,888.84 and the Dow gained 354 points to 51,202.26 on Friday June 12, near records as falling oil and SpaceX's 19% debut pop lifted sentiment; the S&P Equal-Weight and Russell 2000 hit fresh highs. Weekend previews flag the June 16-17 FOMC and Warsh's first press conference, May retail sales and a possible US-Iran signing as the week's catalysts, with a rate hold near-certain.

perspectives.keyFraming: Records into a high-stakes Fed week — broadening leadership is healthy, but hot inflation and an unsigned Iran deal are two-sided risks.
perspectives.summary: Broadening participation, with the equal-weight S&P and small caps at highs, signaled a durable rally, but a hot-inflation Fed meeting and an as-yet-unsigned Iran accord framed the two main risks into June 17.

sources.title: Stock market news for June 12, 2026
Stocks on track for a positive week amid peace hopes

perspectives.keyFraming: A private-sector triumph caps the week: Musk's SpaceX prints the biggest IPO in Wall Street history.
perspectives.summary: CNBC's IPO coverage noted SpaceX (SPCX) jumped 19% to close at $161 in a record $75B debut at a roughly $1.77 trillion implied valuation — a marquee win for American capital markets and a counterpoint to the prior week's chip-driven volatility.

sources.title: SpaceX IPO takeaways: SPCX closes at $161, jumping 19% after record debut

stories.headline: Warsh's first FOMC (June 16-17) seen holding at 3.50-3.75% and dropping its easing bias after May CPI 4.2%, PPI 6.5%
stories.summary: CME FedWatch shows a near-100% chance the Fed holds at new chair Kevin Warsh's debut meeting. The focus is the updated dot plot, Warsh's first press conference and whether projections push the next cut into 2027, after May CPI hit a three-year-high 4.2% (energy +23.5%, gasoline ~+40%) and PPI 6.5%. Trump has publicly warned that a rate increase 'would be wrong,' setting up an early test of Fed independence.

perspectives.keyFraming: A hold is locked in; the story is the new chair's tone and whether the easing bias is dead.
perspectives.summary: With CPI at 4.2% and PPI 6.5%, the base case is a hawkish hold and an explicit move from an easing bias to neutral, with markets watching whether Warsh keeps a later-2026 cut window open or signals the next move is into 2027, and how he handles his first press conference.

sources.title: What To Expect at Kevin Warsh's First Federal Reserve Meeting as Chair
Rising inflation drives rate-cut debate at Warsh's first Fed meeting as chair

stories.headline: Markets abandon 2026 rate-cut bets as hot CPI and PPI keep a Fed hike in play
stories.summary: With May CPI at a three-year-high 4.2% and PPI now at 6.5%, markets have largely abandoned 2026 rate-cut hopes ahead of the June 16-17 FOMC. CME FedWatch prices a near-100% chance the Fed holds the funds target at 3.50-3.75% next week, while leaving open whether at least one 25bp hike comes later in 2026 versus a 2027 cut. The 10-year Treasury yield eased toward 4.47% after Friday's oil drop offered partial relief.

perspectives.keyFraming: A hawkish hold looks likely; the inflation spike is energy-driven and could fade if Hormuz reopens, complicating the hike debate.
perspectives.summary: Futures pricing points to the Fed holding next week while keeping a hike in play later in 2026 after back-to-back hot inflation prints. Analysts note the surge is concentrated in energy tied to the Iran war, so a credible Hormuz-reopening deal could relieve the pressure that is driving rate-hike speculation.

sources.title: Kevin Warsh chairs first Federal Reserve meeting as inflation hits 4%

stories.headline: Treasury yields ease into Fed week as Friday's oil drop cools inflation and hike fears
stories.summary: The 10-year Treasury yield finished Friday June 12 around 4.47-4.49% and the 2-year near 4.09%, with the 10-year down about 10 basis points on the session after Trump signaled a possible weekend US-Iran deal and oil tumbled to a two-month low. The retreat in yields pared earlier bets on a 2026 rate hike that had built after May's 4.2% CPI and 6.5% PPI, leaving the bond market focused on Warsh's June 16-17 FOMC and the new dot plot.

perspectives.keyFraming: Bonds rallied as a possible Iran deal cooled the energy-driven inflation that had revived hike bets.
perspectives.summary: Trading Economics and Advisor Perspectives reported the 10-year hovering near 4.47-4.49% after a roughly 10bp drop, with the move tied to falling oil on US-Iran de-escalation hopes easing inflation and rate-hike concerns ahead of the FOMC.

sources.title: US 10-Year Yield Steadies Ahead of Key Data
Treasury Yields Snapshot: June 12, 2026

stories.headline: Oil sits at a two-month low on US-Iran deal hopes as analysts warn a flow recovery would lag any signing
stories.summary: Crude held its roughly two-month low through the weekend after Friday's settles of WTI $84.88 (down 3.2%) and Brent $87.33 (down 3.4%), WTI's lowest close since late January, as a senior US official put the odds of a US-Iran agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz near 80%. Iran circulated a 14-point draft committing it to reopen the strait within 30 days in exchange for sanctions relief, but Tehran disputed the timing and did not confirm a final deal.

perspectives.keyFraming: Conflicting signals — a president touting an imminent deal against an Iran that will not confirm — keep a war premium in crude.
perspectives.summary: Markets priced roughly 80% odds of an agreement, but the gap between Trump's claims and Tehran's denials kept a war premium in oil, and even a signed memorandum implies a multi-week-to-month physical recovery — mine-clearing, restarting idled fields, repairing facilities — before Hormuz throughput normalizes.

sources.title: Oil prices hold two-month low on hopes of a US-Iran deal despite Tehran pushback
Brent crude oil — Price

perspectives.keyFraming: Trump's pressure-plus-dealmaking is delivering: a war settlement and a reopened chokepoint are within reach.
perspectives.summary: Right-leaning coverage framed the prospective accord as vindication of maximum pressure, with the strait set to reopen and sanctions and frozen-funds relief as the trade, and falling pump prices easing inflation pressure on households.

sources.title: US-Iran deal will likely be signed by the weekend in Geneva – Bloomberg

perspectives.keyFraming: Premature victory laps risk a market head-fake; Tehran holds the real leverage over Hormuz.
perspectives.summary: Left-leaning analysis warned that Iran's repeated denials and added conditions mean a signing is far from assured, and that a stalled deal could snap oil back higher just as the worst inflation in three years bites.

sources.title: Qatari Negotiators Travel To Tehran In Bid To Finalize US-Iran Deal

stories.headline: Bitcoin holds around $63K into the weekend as Fed-week caution and record ETF outflows weigh on crypto
stories.summary: Bitcoin traded around $63,000 into the weekend, pressured by sticky-inflation and rate uncertainty, a firmer dollar and a historic spot-ETF exodus ahead of the June 16-17 FOMC. US spot bitcoin ETFs bled about $3.4 billion in one week — the biggest weekly outflow since the products launched in 2024 — with three straight weeks of redemptions totaling roughly $4.2 billion. Crypto moved on hawkish-Fed fears rather than a crypto-specific catalyst.

perspectives.keyFraming: Crypto trades like a macro asset, selling off on the same hot-inflation, hawkish-Fed fear hitting bonds.
perspectives.summary: The Block and CoinDesk attributed Bitcoin's slide and heavy liquidations to tightening expectations, a record $3.4 billion weekly ETF outflow and a large Strategy sale rather than a crypto-specific shock, with traders positioning cautiously before Warsh's first FOMC decision.

sources.title: 'The rally that wasn't': Bitcoin slides 14% in one week as ETF outflows, Strategy sale and oil prices hit sentiment
Bitcoin ETFs Record Largest-Ever $3.4B Sell-Off

stories.headline: Week ahead: Warsh's first FOMC, May retail sales and a possible US-Iran signing at the G7 set the tone
stories.summary: Markets enter the week near records with three catalysts: the June 16-17 FOMC under new chair Kevin Warsh, where a hold at 3.50-3.75% is near-certain and the dot plot and press conference are the focus; May retail sales, a read on consumer resilience amid 4.2% inflation; and the G7 summit in France (June 15-17), where a US-Iran memorandum to reopen the Strait of Hormuz could be signed. Oil sits at a two-month low and the 10-year yield near 4.47%.

perspectives.keyFraming: A high-stakes, three-headline week: the Fed, the consumer and Middle East diplomacy all land at once.
perspectives.summary: Charles Schwab and CNBC previews framed the week around Warsh's debut FOMC, May retail sales and a potential Iran signing at the G7, with a rate hold near-certain and the market's tone hinging on the dot plot and whether the Hormuz deal actually gets inked.

sources.title: Stocks on track for a positive week amid peace hopes
What To Expect at Kevin Warsh's First Federal Reserve Meeting as Chair

stories.headline: US and Iran reach final-text peace deal; oil holds two-month low as Hormuz reopening eyed within 30 days
stories.summary: Pakistan's PM and Trump said June 14 the US and Iran agreed a final text ending military operations on all fronts, with electronic signing and an official ceremony reported for June 19 in Switzerland and discussion at the G7. The deal commits Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz within 30 days, lift the naval blockade and release about $24bn in frozen funds. Crude held Friday's two-month-low settles (WTI $84.88, Brent $87.33); a US official put the odds near 80-85%.

perspectives.keyFraming: Cautious optimism: a deal is reached, but implementation (demining, field restarts) is the hard part.
perspectives.summary: Bloomberg and CNN reported the US and Iran nearing a finalized deal around the G7, committing Iran to reopen Hormuz within 30 days; analysts cautioned flows will not normalize immediately even after signing.

sources.title: US and Iran Nearing a Peace Deal Around G7 Meeting Next Week
Iran war: US-Iran peace deal updates

perspectives.keyFraming: A Trump diplomatic win heading into the G7, with oil relief flowing to consumers.
perspectives.summary: CNBC framed the deal as a win heading into the G7, with crude near two-month lows on the prospect of Hormuz reopening and the naval blockade lifted.

sources.title: Trump heads to G7 with Iran deal and Ukraine on the agenda

stories.headline: Warsh's first FOMC (June 16-17): a hold is near-certain as the new dot plot and inflation tone take center stage
stories.summary: CME FedWatch shows about 98% odds the Fed holds at 3.50-3.75% at Kevin Warsh's debut meeting. The market focus is the new dot plot and Warsh's tone: analysts expect projections to show no further 2026 cuts, with some officials possibly signaling a hike, after May CPI hit 4.2% (core 2.9%) and PPI 6.5%. Warsh is seen treading carefully — policy effectively on hold but ready to hike if needed.

perspectives.keyFraming: Hold plus a neutral pivot, with the dot plot the real signal at Warsh's debut.
perspectives.summary: Chase and other previews expect a hold at 3.50-3.75% with projections likely showing no further 2026 cuts, as the new chair treads carefully after hot May CPI and PPI readings.

sources.title: What To Expect at Kevin Warsh's First Federal Reserve Meeting as Chair

perspectives.keyFraming: Stagflation risk: markets test the new chair via a Treasury selloff.
perspectives.summary: Market commentary framed Warsh's first meeting as a test amid sticky inflation, with some analysts warning of stagflation risk and a possible hawkish hold or hike signal.

sources.title: Warsh Faces His First Test June 17 as Traders Hunt for Signals in the Fed's Dot Plot

stories.headline: Crude holds a two-month low into a heavy week of Fed, IEA report and Iran-deal implementation
stories.summary: WTI settled $84.88 and Brent $87.33 Friday (both about two-month lows, down 3-4%) on Iran-deal optimism. The week ahead brings the FOMC, the IEA monthly report and Philly Fed manufacturing. The EIA STEO still models Brent near $105 in June-July if Hormuz stays largely shut, while OPEC cut its 2026 demand-growth forecast to about 970k bpd on soft Chinese demand. Analysts warn even a signed deal will not normalize flows immediately.

perspectives.keyFraming: Relief is priced in, but supply stays physically constrained until the strait actually reopens.
perspectives.summary: CNBC reported crude at two-month lows on Iran-deal hopes while the EIA STEO still models Brent near $105 if Hormuz stays shut and OPEC trimmed demand-growth forecasts on weak Chinese buying.

sources.title: Oil prices ease as US-Iran deal hopes build despite Tehran pushback

perspectives.keyFraming: Scenario-driven: a failed deal could snap prices back above $95.
perspectives.summary: Market analysis framed the week as scenario-dependent, warning that a collapse of the US-Iran agreement could quickly revive the war premium and push crude back above $95.

sources.title: Crude oil weekly outlook: scenarios for US-Iran negotiation outcomes
